title: CLI write-path UX — actionable AJV validation errors + --show-schema field hint: root cause, fix seams, and existing-filing map
question: What is the root cause of (1) non-actionable pi-context validation errors and (2) the absence of an inline block-field preview on write ops; where do the fixes live; and are they already filed?
method: Two read-only Explore code investigations tracing the write/validation path (the ValidationError constructor + the validate funnels) and the schema-derivation surface (read-schema/readSchema/resolveBlockItemSchema + the block_kinds array_key mapping), plus substrate read ops (driven through the global pi-context command) enumerating existing gaps/tasks, cross-checked against the live CLI.
findings_summary:
Two write-path frictions in the pi-context CLI, both grounded to root cause and both ALREADY FILED (FGAP-023 -> TASK-016; FGAP-022 -> TASK-017, cli-parity release-group orders 2/5 and 3/5; do not refile).

(1) Non-actionable validation errors. The string 'Validation failed for <block>: <instancePath>: <message>' is built at one shared site — the ValidationError constructor, schema-validator.ts:88 — which maps each AJV ErrorObject to instancePath+message and DISCARDS e.params. AJV runs with allErrors:true (schema-validator.ts:13) and the constructor both receives and stores the full errors[] on this.errors (:92), so the actionable data is present but dropped: params.additionalProperty names the offending field (e.g. 'reproduction'), params.missingProperty the missing required field, params.allowedValues the enum set. Every write op (append/update/upsert/nested block items, write-block, append-relation, amend-config, schema writes) funnels through this one constructor via validate()/validateFromFile()/validateBlockWithMigrationForDir()/validateSchemaAgainstMeta() — so a single change makes all of them actionable. The CLI is a pure err.message passthrough (cli.ts:511-523, bin.ts:8-9) reading neither err.errors nor params. Two fix locations: (a) library-side at schema-validator.ts:88 — fixes every surface incl. the in-pi pi -p tool dispatch, but changes the in-pi op message; (b) CLI-side reading err.errors[].params in the cli.ts catch — keeps in-pi byte-unchanged. TASK-016 chooses (b): lift formatAjvError from scripts/orchestrator/file-block-item.ts:184-205 into the catch + a new render.ts.

(2) No inline block-field preview. The derivation primitive already exists: read-schema (ops-registry.ts:1140-1181) -> readSchema (schema-write.ts:327) addresses any schema node via --path; resolveBlockItemSchema (block-api.ts:2266-2295) returns {arrayKey, itemSchema}, resolving the array property + items $ref. The friction is a 3-step indirection: know schemaName==block; resolve array_key from config.block_kinds (read-config --registry block_kinds, ops-registry.ts:896-942); then read-schema --path properties.<array_key>.items and hand-read required+properties. --show-schema (TASK-017) collapses this into one early-return branch in main() mirroring the parsed.help branch (cli.ts:469-472), before op.run, reusing readConfig+readSchema+resolveBlockItemSchema — confined to pi-context-cli/src/cli.ts.

The two are sibling/complementary (reactive error-time vs proactive pre-write), both from the 2026-06-05 cli-ops-scripts-parity-survey (GAP-5, GAP-4), both honoring 'in-pi ops byte-unchanged'. Open decision: the in-pi pi -p tool surface keeps raw AJV under the CLI-scoped fix; covering it requires the library-side option (a) at schema-validator.ts:88, which TASK-016 deliberately avoids to keep in-pi output stable.
stale_conditions: schema-validator.ts ValidationError message builder (:88) is changed to surface AJV params — the root-cause claim is realized.
TASK-016 / TASK-017 land — the 'not yet implemented' framing and the library-vs-CLI fix-location choice are superseded by the shipped behavior.
cli.ts dispatch/catch region is restructured (the :469-472 help-branch seam or :511-523 catch) — the seam file:line references drift.
read-schema / resolveBlockItemSchema / read-config block_kinds derivation surface changes — the --show-schema reuse path shifts.

citations.label: validation-error message collapse (root cause) — maps to instancePath+message, discards params
AJV allErrors:true; full errors[] stored on this.errors
CLI is pure err.message passthrough (reads neither errors nor params)
read-schema op — on-demand schema-node addressing (derivation primitive)
--show-schema seam: parsed.help early-return branch in main()
